title: Venezuela Russia And The Oil Balance
section_path: Venezuela Russia And The Oil Balance
section_path_text: Venezuela Russia And The Oil Balance
summary:
Main
Interpreting Current Signals for Tanker Demand
Renewed attention on Venezuela's oil sector has re-entered market discussion alongside the continued geopolitical reshaping of global crude trade flows. For freight markets, the relevance of these developments lies less in oil reserves potential or political signalling and more in how existing and incremental barrels interact with supply-demand balances, price structure, and established trade routes. Venezuela: reserves versus freight-relevant reality
Venezuela holds one of the world's largest proven crude oil reserves.

children.title: Contango and storage economics
children.summary: Price structure matters as much as absolute price levels. Contango describes a forward curve in which futures prices for later delivery trade above the prompt or spot price, reflecting the cost of carrying oil over time, including storage, financing, insurance, and freight.
